source.title: An integrative theory of locus coeruleus-norepinephrine function: adaptive gain and optimal performance

confidence_rationale: The phasic-vs-tonic LC framework gives a principled architecture for the broadcast trigger / accumulator transition. Phasic bursts on single events; sustained tonic shifts on accumulated utility decline. ACC/OFC inputs to LC put the trigger under cortical control. This is the most architecturally complete LC paper for the V_s purpose -- it specifies both the trigger event (phasic) and the integrated state (tonic) that together implement the trigger-plus-accumulator structure MECH-284 needs.
failure_signatures: Disrupted phasic-tonic balance produces dissociable cognitive failures: hyper-tonic LC (anxiety, hypervigilance) is over-invalidation, where the system is constantly searching for alternative routines; hypo-tonic LC (depression, dementia) is under-invalidation, where stale routines persist. The framework predicts that V_s accumulator integrity depends on the relative weighting of phasic vs tonic LC components and that this weighting is itself under cortical control via ACC/OFC.

mapping.source_claim_statement: LC neurons exhibit two modes -- phasic and tonic -- that map to exploitation (phasic LC, focused on current task) and exploration (tonic LC, search for alternative behaviours). The mode is controlled by frontal inputs (ACC, OFC) that monitor task-related utility. Phasic activity facilitates ensuing behaviours and optimises task performance; tonic activity drives disengagement and behavioural flexibility.
mapping.ree_translation: The phasic-tonic LC framework is the cleanest existing biological substrate for the trigger-plus-accumulator structure MECH-284 needs. Phasic burst = single broadcast invalidation event tagged to the currently committed behaviour. Tonic LC level = integrated state reflecting accumulated utility decline of the currently operative anchor / behavioural mode. When tonic LC crosses threshold, the system disengages from the operative anchor (MECH-269 reset) and explores alternatives (probe-dominant routing, refining MECH-272). ACC and OFC inputs to LC are the credit-assignment circuits that decide which schema's failures should drive the tonic shift.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The framework was developed in the context of exploration-exploitation in single-task settings, not for hippocampal-anchor or regional-schema invalidation. The translation 'tonic LC = V_s accumulator output' is structural; the LC tonic shift is generic ('the current task is not yielding utility') rather than schema-specific ('the current anchor is not fitting the regional dynamics'). Schema-specificity would require either targeted LC projections (some evidence for) or local credit assignment at the receivers (consistent with ACC/OFC inputs).
mapping.source_context: Theoretical review with macaque LC electrophysiology and computational modelling; senior author Aston-Jones is a leading LC investigator.

confidence_components.notes: Annu Rev Neurosci, foundational review. Mapping high because the phasic-tonic structure maps directly to trigger-plus-accumulator. Transfer low because LC function is conserved.
